# ppmf

The goal of `ppmf` is to convert Census Privacy Protected Microdata
Files into somewhat wider data aggregated to a geographic level.

## Installation

To install the stable version from CRAN, use the following code:

``` r

install.packages('ppmf')
```

You can install the development version from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r

# install.packages('remotes')
remotes::install_github('christopherkenny/ppmf')
```

## Basics

Load the package:

``` r

library(ppmf)
```

Download and read data with:

``` r

path <- download_ppmf(dsn = 'filename.csv', dir = 'some/directory', version = '19')
al <- read_ppmf(state = 'AL', path = path)
```

Version ‘19’ reflects the 19.61 finalized parameters used again for the
2020 Census.

For future use, I recommend storing the path to the data for future
sessions using:

``` r

add_pmmf19_path(path)
```

Then the path can be recovered with:

``` r

path19 <- Sys.getenv('ppmf19')
```

Once you’ve read in what you want, you can aggregate it to the right
level:

``` r

al <- al |> add_geoid()
blocks <- agg(al)
```

And aggregated data can use the GEOID to merge with shapefiles:

``` r

library(dplyr) # to clean up the data

shp <- tigris::blocks('AL', year = 2010) |> 
  select(GEOID10, geometry) |> rename(GEOID = GEOID10)
shp <- shp |> left_join(blocks, by = 'GEOID')

# always clean shp!
shp[is.na(shp)] <- 0
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

For users with the newest package version, there is an added dependency
on [`censable`](https://christophertkenny.com/censable/), which allows
for an easier workflow. If you’ve used the `add_pmmf*_path()` workflow
suggested, you don’t even need to supply the paths!

This will not just read the `ppmf` data, it will merge it with 2010
Census populations (by major race/ethnicity grouping) and add the
corresponding geometries.

``` r

al <- read_merge_ppmf('AL', level = 'block', versions = '19')
```

# Package index

## Get and Read Data

Functions to download data, locate data urls, and read in data

- [`download_ppmf()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/download_ppmf.md)
  : Download PPMF Files
- [`get_ppmf_links()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/get_ppmf_links.md)
  : Get PPMF File Links
- [`read_merge_ppmf()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/read_merge_ppmf.md)
  : Read PPMF data and Merge with Census 2010 Data
- [`read_ppmf()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/read_ppmf.md)
  : Read in PPMF Data

## Save Paths for Future Use

Tools to save environmental variables

- [`add_ppmf12_path()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/add_ppmf12_path.md)
  : Add ppmf12 path to Renviron
- [`add_ppmf19_path()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/add_ppmf19_path.md)
  : Add ppmf19 path to Renviron
- [`add_ppmf19r_path()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/add_ppmf19r_path.md)
  : Add ppmf19r path to Renviron
- [`add_ppmf4_path()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/add_ppmf4_path.md)
  : Add ppmf4 path to Renviron

## Work with PPMF Data

Basic tools to work with PPMF data

- [`agg()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/agg.md) :
  Aggregate PPMF Data
- [`overwrite_hisp_race()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/overwrite_hisp_race.md)
  : Overwrite Races with Hispanic
- [`replace_race()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/replace_race.md)
  : Replace Race Categories

## Work with Census style GEOIDs

Use Census geographic identifiers

- [`add_geoid()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/add_geoid.md)
  : Add Standard GEOID to PPMF Data
- [`breakdown_geoid()`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/breakdown_geoid.md)
  : Breakdown GEOID into Components

## Included Data

Data included for examples and to clarify groupings

- [`ppmf_ex`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/ppmf_ex.md)
  : Example PPMF Data
- [`races`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/races.md)
  : Race Classifications
- [`states`](https://christopherkenny.github.io/ppmf/reference/states.md)
  : State Rows

## Misc Functions

Remaining funcctions
